flags.concern: video-editor-lightbox-navigation: the plan still anchors navigation to `lightboxAssetKey`, but this editor can have multiple clips pointing at the same asset key. `ClipAction` opens the lightbox with only `clipMeta.asset`, `VideoEditorProvider` stores only that asset key, and duplicate-clip flows preserve the same asset on the new clip. If the executor follows step 1 literally and 'finds the clip that references the current lightboxAssetKey via meta', repeated assets will resolve to an arbitrary/first matching clip, so next/previous ordering and the shot-group indicator can start from the wrong timeline item.
flags.evidence: Only `assetKey` is stored in [VideoEditorProvider.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx:87) and opened from [ClipAction.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:402). Duplicate clips reuse the same asset in [duplicate-clip.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/duplicate-clip.ts:32), and repeated-asset configs are already covered by [timeline-save-utils.test.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.test.ts:320).

flags.concern: media-lightbox-navigation-state: step 3 is under-scoped for the shot-group badge. Extending `LightboxNavigationProps` alone is not enough here: the current lightbox pipeline strips navigation down to `showNavigation`/`hasNext`/`hasPrevious` plus handlers before `NavigationArrows` renders, so badge fields like `groupLabel`, `groupColor`, or counters will never arrive unless the builders, context shape, and render models are updated too. On top of that, the proposed hook inputs do not include shot names, and `PinnedShotGroup` stores only `shotId`, so the plan does not currently explain how it will produce the must-have human-readable shot label.
flags.evidence: Navigation is narrowed in [lightboxSharedBuilders.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/lightboxSharedBuilders.ts:55) and [lightboxSharedBuilders.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/lightboxSharedBuilders.ts:119), the context shape only exposes those fields in [LightboxStateContext.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/domains/media-lightbox/contexts/LightboxStateContext.tsx:81), and the render models forward only the reduced state in [ImageLightbox.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/domains/media-lightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:257) and [useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/videoLightbox/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:117). `NavigationArrows` itself has no props for badge metadata in [NavigationArrows.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/domains/media-lightbox/components/NavigationArrows.tsx:6). Separately, `PinnedShotGroup` has only `shotId` in [types/index.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts:172), while `useShotGroups()` needs `shots` data to derive display names in [useShotGroups.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:28).
